title: Fiery Crispy Mexican Onion Tacos Al Pastor
description: A peppery, crispy, and cozy Mexican dish featuring onion, made in a claypot and served as tacos al pastor, this recipe is nut-free-friendly and perfect for a dinner gathering.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large claypot over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the dried arbol chilies, orange juice, lime juice, salt, and black pepper to the claypot and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Using a mortar and pestle, pound the onion and chili mixture until it is coarsely chopped.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the onion and chili mixture onto a tortilla and topping with chopped cilantro.
Serve the tacos immediately and enjoy.
